# the yeomanizer

> A free, unofficial web tool that drafts U.S. Navy correspondence — standard naval letters,
> memoranda, endorsements, and NATO travel orders — in the exact format prescribed by SECNAV
> M-5216.5, with a live preview and in-browser .docx / .pdf / .json export.

the yeomanizer formats naval correspondence automatically: the letterhead, the
SSIC/identification block, the From/To/Via/Subj/Ref/Encl heading block, automatic paragraph
numbering and indentation, optional underlined section titles, the signature block, CUI
markings, and multi-page pagination — all to the manual's standard. You type plain text on one
side and a correctly formatted letter appears on the other.

## Key facts
- Free, and runs entirely in your web browser. No account or login.
- Privacy by design: the tool stores and transmits nothing — your draft lives only in the browser
  tab and is erased when you close it. You download and handle the exported files yourself.
- Unofficial — not affiliated with or endorsed by the U.S. Navy or the Department of Defense, and
  not an official system of record. It makes no authorization claim; CUI belongs only on authorized
  Government-Furnished Equipment.
- Three exports, all generated in the browser: a pixel-accurate vector/searchable PDF with a
  CAC-signable signature field; an editable Microsoft Word (.docx) document; and a small plain-text
  .json copy of the draft that can be re-imported to keep working. The exported documents are clean
  — no hidden or embedded data. It can also combine a saved letter PDF with enclosure files into a
  single packet.
- Document types: standard naval letter, memorandum (From-To), endorsements (appended to a
  letter automatically when a Via addressee is added, or built standalone), and the NATO
  travel order (the bilingual two-page form).
- Also handles CUI markings, inline emphasis (bold, italic, underline), automatic U.S.-grade to
  NATO (OF/OR) rank translation, letterhead in printed / plain-paper / pre-printed modes with the
  DoD seal, and a layout that works on phones, tablets, and desktops.

## Where
- https://yeomanizer.com

## Who it's for
Sailors, Marines, and DoD civilians who need to produce correctly formatted naval
correspondence quickly without memorizing SECNAV M-5216.5.
